claim: Conjecture B4 (sharp WKB exponent identity, A=2d) sharpened from v1.1 verified-at-d=3 (50/50) to v1.2 verified-at-d in {3,4} (110/110 jointly): cubic mean A_fit=5.978 sigma=0.026, quartic mean A_fit=7.954 sigma=0.0037; PCF-2 v1.2 sec B4 + Q1-quartic

Universal Newton-polygon characteristic root xi_0(b)=d/beta_d^(1/d) with c(d)=d: verified at d=4 to spread 0 (relative <1e-50) across 8 representative quartics; recovers proven d=2 case xi_0=2/sqrt(beta_2); falsifies the v1.1 Channel-Theory candidate c(d)=2*sqrt((d-1)!) which gives c(4)~4.899 vs measured 4

d=2 anomaly status (op:d2-anomaly): the {2d-1, 2d} A-branch split is observed only at d=2 across the joint cubic (50/50) + quartic (60/60) lex-window catalogue; degrees 3 and 4 populate only the sharp branch A=2d

log|Delta_3| flagged as borderline candidate finer-cubic-split slow-trend invariant: Spearman rho=-0.451 (full 50), rho=-0.492 (excluding loose-fit family 31), Bonferroni-corrected p in [3.7e-3, 1.1e-2] over m=11 valid tests; signal weakens under inverse-variance weighting (|rho|_w=0.26); R1 HALT threshold (|rho|>0.6 at Bonferroni p<1e-3) NOT triggered; Conjecture B4 retained

PCF-2 v1.2 supersedes v1.1: 18-page PDF compiled in 3 pdflatex passes with 0 errors and 0 undefined references; concept DOI preserved (10.5281/zenodo.19936297); folds in Q1-A, Q1-B, R1; v1.1 archived to tex/submitted/archive/pcf2_program_statement_v1.1.tex
